commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: And they were filled with fury; and discussed one with another what they might do to Jesus. Cornelius a Lapide And they were filled with madness. α̉νοίας, deprived of understanding, they could not answer Him a word; they were filled with anger because they could not gainsay the reasoning of Christ, and with envy, as the Syriac renders it, which was the cause of their madness. Their eyes were blinded so that they could not see the truth. Hence Francis Lucas adds, they communed one with another what they might do with Jesus, i. how they might make away with Him.
